headline: Melaka Tourism Revenue Falls 18% Despite RM2.1B Investment
context: Melaka's tourism revenue declined 18% to RM8.4 billion in 2025 despite RM2.1 billion in state and federal tourism infrastructure investment since 2020, as overtourism backlash, traffic congestion, and rising accommodation costs deterred repeat visitors.

cards.big: RM2.1 billion invested in Melaka tourism but revenue fell 18% — more infrastructure didn't mean more visitors
cards.sub: Average tourist spending dropped from RM680 to RM520 per visit as budget travellers replaced high-spenders

cards.big: Melaka attracted 14.2 million visitors in 2025 but per-visitor revenue fell 28% from 2019 levels
cards.sub: Hotel room oversupply at 42,000 rooms pushed average rates down 22% — infrastructure investment outpaced demand growth

cards.big: Heritage zone residents report 340% increase in noise complaints and 45% decline in quality of life since 2018
cards.sub: Local businesses displaced by tourist-oriented shops — traditional trades like tin-smithing and batik-making priced out of Jonker Street

cards.big: Tourism tax collected RM28 million from Melaka but only RM4.2 million returned to heritage conservation
cards.sub: No carrying capacity study conducted despite UNESCO World Heritage Site status requiring visitor management plans

cards.big: When tourism investment grows but revenue shrinks, the problem isn't spending enough — it's spending on the wrong things
Should Melaka implement a visitor cap for the heritage zone based on UNESCO carrying capacity assessment?
